steps.title: Identify the passage's concluding emphasis
steps.content: The passage defines horizontal gene transfer, notes it is common among prokaryotes, and then focuses on a consequence: increased antibiotic resistance. The final sentence reveals the main point: understanding how to prevent HGT “might result in the mitigation of dangerous pathogens.” The passage builds to a practical conclusion—why studying HGT matters.

steps.title: Match the emphasis to the answer
steps.content: Choice A captures this practical emphasis: study of HGT may yield useful applications. Choices B, C, and D claim the passage compares HGT's prevalence to earlier views or presents new research findings, but the passage makes no such comparisons or claims. That matches choice A — A is correct.
